title: microcos subepetala
intro: Microcos subepetala, also known as the Borneo subepetala or Malayan subepetala, is a species of flowering plant in the family Malvaceae. It is native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia, where it is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating pain, and supporting immune function. Microcos subepetala is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by extracting the plant's leaves, stems, and roots in hot water or a solvent like ethanol or glycerin.
